visible_labels: Start a fresh benchmark run or load saved benchmark runs to see which backend or review setup performed better across representative sample projects.
Start a fresh benchmark run or load saved benchmark runs to see which backend or review setup performed better across representative sample projects.

Use this page in three steps:
1. Start a new benchmark with Run Benchmarks, or pick a saved run from the list below and load it as the main run.
2. Optionally load a second saved run to compare against it.
3. Read the takeaway summary first, then use the scenario table below to see where the difference came from.

Use this page in three steps:
1. Start a new benchmark with Run Benchmarks, or pick a saved run from the list below and load it as the main run.
2. Optionally load a second saved run to compare against it.
3. Read the takeaway summary first, then use the scenario table below to see where the difference came from.
Most users start by running a fresh benchmark or loading a saved run from an earlier session. The built-in sample scenarios are there if you want to inspect what each benchmark is testing.
Most users start by running a fresh benchmark or loading a saved run from an earlier session. The built-in sample scenarios are there if you want to inspect what each benchmark is testing.
Most people only need Run Benchmarks, the saved-run selector, and the main compare buttons below. Expand the advanced section only if you want to change folders or open raw files.
